plainEnglish: Any feature you can operate with a multi-finger or path-based gesture (pinch-zoom, swipe, drag along a path) must also work with a single tap or click. Many people can't perform complex gestures, so provide simple single-pointer alternatives like buttons.
whyItMatters: Users with motor disabilities, or those using a head pointer or switch, can't perform multipoint or path-based gestures like pinch or swipe and need a single-pointer alternative.
quickCheck: For every swipe, pinch, or path gesture, confirm there's a simple single-tap/click control (such as buttons) that does the same thing.
commonMistakes: Carousels or galleries that can only be advanced by swiping, with no previous/next buttons.
Pinch-to-zoom as the only way to zoom, with no zoom buttons.
Path-based gestures (like drawing a shape) with no single-pointer equivalent.

codeExamples.label: Swipe plus single-pointer buttons
Advancing requires a swipe gesture
codeExamples.code:
{/* Only advances via touch swipe — no button alternative */}
{/* slides */}
